summary: Iteration 6 CRITICAL balance-sheet correctness testing: Verified debit-normal double-entry engine on Skyward Sparks seed. Trial Balance balanced (total_debit == total_credit), Balance Sheet balanced=true imbalance=0.0, Income Statement Net Income matches BS 'Current Period Net Income' equity line, GL sum-per-account == TB signed balance across all accounts. Books remain balanced after (a) manual JE, (b) manual transaction (auto-defaulted to bank 1010), (c) split transaction. A/R Aging endpoint returns 5-bucket shape; sidebar drill-in + A/R aging widget UI both work. 8/8 iter6 tests + 57/57 regression = 65/65 backend pass.
action_items: Startup-blocker fix applied by tester (server.py had ~14 lines of duplicated garbage after `pass` on line 1708 causing SyntaxError -> backend 502). Main agent should audit for accidental duplicate merge blocks in future.

server.py::create_transaction referenced `bank_id` without defining it -> 500 on every manual transaction POST. Tester patched to compute bank_id from inp.bank_account_id and auto-default to code '1010' if missing (this is exactly the review-requested behavior; both auto-default AND split tests now pass).
critical_code_review_comments: reports.py debit-normal signed-balance engine is correct: on the seed dataset Trial Balance is $69,800 debit = $69,800 credit; Balance Sheet A=$60,959.09 = L+E=$60,959.09 (imbalance=0.0). Journal entries are now included in _signed_balances() alongside transactions -- confirmed by posting a $250 JE and observing tb/bs still balanced.

server.py had TWO defects blocking iter6 acceptance until fixed by tester: (1) ~14 duplicate/corrupted lines appended after line 1708 breaking uvicorn startup; (2) `bank_id` variable was never defined inside create_transaction despite being referenced 3x. Both are simple integration errors, not architectural. Please review E1's diff review process to catch these.
Sidebar.jsx inAccounting state persists based on route (/accounting/*) which is correct; Back button clears state and returns to main menu without route change. Clean UX.
Invoices.jsx A/R aging widget renders 5 buckets with distinct colors + a stacked visual bar; bucket sums equal outstanding A/R total ($5,400 on seed, all in Current bucket).
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py (removed duplicate garbage tail lines 1709-1722; added bank_id resolution with auto-default to '1010' inside create_transaction)
seed_data_creation: Test suite created a small number of TEST_iter6_* transactions and one $250 TEST_iter6_JE journal entry against the Skyward Sparks company. Books remain balanced with these residuals; no cleanup required for correctness.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The balance-sheet correctness engine in reports.py is verified good on this seed. If the seed is re-run, re-invoke /app/backend/tests/test_iteration6_balance.py -- all 8 assertions are seed-amount-agnostic (they assert balanced=True and per-account GL == TB rather than specific dollar amounts). If create_transaction throws 500 again with NameError bank_id, the tester's patch was reverted -- reapply the auto-default block around server.py:504.
rca of the issue: Root cause of iter6 acceptance blockers: (1) server.py had a corrupted duplicate tail (lines 1709-1722) starting with `s - %(message)s")` causing SyntaxError and backend 502 on startup. Fixed by truncating to line 1708. (2) create_transaction referenced `bank_id` variable that was never assigned -- likely a partial edit where inp.bank_account_id -> bank_id rename was left incomplete. Fixed by adding `bank_id = inp.bank_account_id` and auto-default lookup for code '1010' when missing (this is what the review request said was implemented but wasn't wired correctly). After both fixes, Balance Sheet, Trial Balance, GL and Income Statement all reconcile within 2 cents in every scenario tested.
